input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for their .NET project. They haven't run coverage yet, so I need to:

1. Explore the project structure
2. Set up and run tests with code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

data.content: Now I have a full picture. Let me run the tests with coverage collection.
data.reasoningText: Good, I now have a clear picture of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage using coverlet (which is already referenced in the test project). I'll use the `dotnet test` command with coverage collection enabled and output in Cobertura format, then parse the results.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-2095bb742c23454d95f6d4f0ac06eb10 && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./TestResults -- DataCollectionRunSettings.DataCollectors.DataCollector.Configuration.Format=cobertura 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with code coverage", "initial_wait": 60}
